skill.name: firebase-ai-logic
skill.description: Guides developers through adding Firebase AI Logic and Gemini-powered features to Firebase applications, including SDK setup, prompts, streaming, multimodal examples, and basic security rules.
skill.summary: Add Gemini-powered Firebase AI Logic features to web and app projects.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static findings are largely false positives caused by Markdown code fences, placeholder Firebase configuration, and documentation links. The skill contains no runnable script, hidden automation, prompt injection text, credential exfiltration logic, or obfuscated payload. Low residual risk remains because it instructs users to run Firebase and npm CLI commands and to configure API credentials.

low_findings.title: User-Run CLI Commands in Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill tells users to install Firebase tools, log in, initialize Firebase, and install npm packages. These are legitimate setup steps, but users should review package sources and understand local project changes before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit in Markdown code fences and would affect the user environment if copied. They are ordinary Firebase setup commands, not hidden or automatically executed by the skill.

low_findings.title: Credential Handling Guidance Requires Care
low_findings.description: The skill shows placeholder Firebase config values and a .env.local project structure entry. It also explicitly says not to hardcode API keys and to use environment variables, so this is guidance rather than credential harvesting.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key is a placeholder string and the file reference appears in a proposed project tree. There is no code that reads, collects, or transmits existing environment files.

low_findings.title: Network References Are Expected for Firebase AI Integration
low_findings.description: The skill includes an image URL fetch helper in an example and links to Firebase and Gemini documentation. These references are expected for an AI integration guide and do not show exfiltration intent.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only hardcoded URLs are public documentation links, and the fetch-related example depends on a user-provided image URL. No destination for secret exfiltration is present.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Findings Dismissed After Context Review
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and dangerous-combination heuristic findings are not supported by the reviewed content. No cryptographic algorithm, host reconnaissance, obfuscation, prompt injection, or automatic code execution was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is descriptive metadata and line 115 is a Firebase security rule using request.auth.uid. The combined heuristic is based on unrelated documentation examples rather than executable malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Adding Gemini features to Firebase apps requires correct SDK setup, prompt design, and safety controls. This skill gives step-by-step guidance for Firebase AI Logic integration and secure implementation patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Firebase AI Logic for text generation, summarization, translation, and image analysis.
Provides Firebase CLI setup steps and SDK installation guidance for web projects.
Shows Firebase AI initialization with getAI and getGenerativeModel.
Outlines text generation, streaming response, and multimodal image analysis examples.
Includes basic Firestore security rule guidance for AI request logs.
Lists safety constraints for API keys, user input validation, rate limiting, and sensitive data handling.
limitations: The skill is a short integration guide, not a complete production architecture.
Examples focus on web JavaScript and TypeScript usage.
It does not provide detailed cost monitoring, abuse detection, or compliance workflows.
The examples use placeholders and require project-specific Firebase configuration.

use_cases.title: Add AI to a Firebase Web App
use_cases.description: Use the setup steps and initialization pattern to connect a Firebase app to Gemini through Firebase AI Logic.

use_cases.title: Prototype Streaming AI Responses
use_cases.description: Use the streaming example to design chat, assistant, or live content generation features.

use_cases.title: Review Firebase AI Safety Controls
use_cases.description: Use the security rules and constraints to guide API key handling, input validation, and rate limiting decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Firebase AI Logic
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the firebase-ai-logic skill to help me add Firebase AI Logic to my existing Firebase web app. Explain the setup steps and files I need to change.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Text Generation Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the firebase-ai-logic skill to design a Firebase AI Logic text generation feature for my app. Include initialization, prompt handling, and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Streaming AI Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the firebase-ai-logic skill to plan a streaming Gemini response flow in Firebase. Include client state handling, loading states, and failure recovery.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Firebase AI Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the firebase-ai-logic skill to review my Firebase AI Logic implementation for API key exposure, unvalidated input, unlimited usage, and unsafe data sharing.

output_examples.input: Add Firebase AI Logic to a web app that summarizes user notes.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan covering Firebase setup, SDK installation, model initialization, prompt structure, response handling, and basic rate limiting.

output_examples.input: Review my AI image analysis idea for Firebase.
output_examples.output: A feature checklist that covers accepted image sources, prompt format, Firebase AI Logic calls, error paths, and sensitive data restrictions.

output_examples.input: Improve my Firebase AI security rules.
output_examples.output: A focused review of authentication checks, user-owned AI logs, create permissions, and missing controls for abuse prevention.

best_practices: Keep Firebase API keys and project configuration out of committed source when secrets or private values are involved.
Validate user input and avoid sending sensitive personal data to AI models.
Add rate limits, caching, and error handling before exposing AI features to production users.
anti_patterns: Do not copy placeholder API keys or project identifiers into production code.
Do not allow unlimited AI calls from unauthenticated or untrusted users.
Do not treat generated AI responses as trusted data without validation and review.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps you add Firebase AI Logic features such as text generation, streaming output, and image analysis.

faq.question: Does it support Gemini in Firebase?
faq.answer: Yes. The guide focuses on Firebase AI Logic with Gemini model initialization and usage examples.

faq.question: Is this a complete production template?
faq.answer: No. It is an integration guide and needs project-specific architecture, testing, monitoring, and security review.

faq.question: Does the skill run Firebase commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents commands for the user to run after reviewing their project and environment.

faq.question: How should API keys be handled?
faq.answer: Use project-appropriate environment configuration and avoid committing sensitive values to source control.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
